question_text: Two springs, A and B, are tested. Spring A requires 5 N to extend by 0.1 m, while spring B requires 10 N to extend by 0.2 m. Which spring is stiffer and by how much? Show your working.
correct_answer: Spring A has k = 50 N/m; Spring B has k = 50 N/m. Both springs have the same spring constant, so neither is stiffer than the other.
explanation: For spring A: k = F/e = 5/0.1 = 50 N/m. For spring B: k = F/e = 10/0.2 = 50 N/m. Although spring B needs more force to extend, it extends more, so the spring constants are equal. A higher spring constant means a stiffer spring.
